english: though the thing does not exist in reality; *ni méd-na yaṅ yoṅ dug* (ni med na yang yong dug) the '*ni*' ('ni') may be dispensed with, though '*ni*' ('ni') be omitted, it will be all right; rgyá-la méd-pai yi-ge drug (rgya la med pai yi ge drug) Glr. six letters not existing in Sanskrit; méd-kyaṅ-ruṅ-baí yig-bru g̥cig (med kyang rung ba'i yig bru gcig) a letter that may also be wanting, a dispensable letter, e.g. པྱ (pya) Glr.; méd-kyaṅ dgós-pai k̥ral-bsdúd (med kyang dgos pai khral bsdud) Mil. a taxation necessary, and even if one possesses nothing, yet as it were inexorable; méd-pa (med pa) (W. *méd-k̥an* (*med kan*)) not being, not exist-ing, not having; blá-ma-la bźúgs-grogs méd-pa lágs-sam (bla ma la bzhugs grogs med pa lags sam) Mil. has your Reverence no fellow-resident in your house? fem. méd-ma (med ma) Mil.; W. *mí dud-pa-méd-k̥an* (*mi dud pa med kan*) very or quite smokeless; mi brnáṅs-pa skyúg-tu méd-pa míd-du méd-pa (mi brnangs pa skyug tu med pa mid du med pa) Dzl. a man about to be choked, being neither able to spit out, nor to swallow down; bdag (bdag) (or bdag-lu (bdag lu)) ċaṅ dbul-du med (cang dbul du med) Dzl. we are not able to give any thing; med-mi-ruṅ-gí bu-tsá (med mi rung gi bu tsha) Mil. the sons and grandsons that are to get something (as a heritage); k̥yím der méd-du mi gtúb-pa (khyim der med du mi gtub pa), or mi ruṅ-ba (mi rung ba) indis-pensable in the house Thgy.; so also med-ťabs-méd-pai blón-po (med thabs med pai blon po) Glr.; méd-par gyúr-ba (med par gyur ba) to be annihilated, to disappear, stág-mo méd-par gyúr-to (stag mo med par gyur to) Pth. the tigress disap-peared; gnam daṅ sa yaṅ med-gyur-na (gnam dang sa yang med gyur na) Dzl. when heaven and earth shall pass away; *da ṅa ċaṅ méd-k̥an soṅ* (*da nga cang med kan song*) W. now I am quite undone; blón-po-rnams gran-sems-méd-par gyur-to (blon po rnams gran sems med par gyur to) Glr. the ministers lost their litigiousness, gave up quarreling; zas brím-du méd-par gyúr-to (zas brim du med par gyur to) Dzl. the distribution of the dishes became impossible; *pé-ra zér-ce méd-k̥an soṅ* (*pe ra zer ce med kan song*) W. he became speech-less; med-par byéd-pa (med par byed pa) to annihilate, an enemy Dzl., to put an end to, a quarrel Glr.; frq. méd-pa(r) (med pa(r)) may be rendered by 'with-out': rgyál-po źig méd-na mi ruṅ (rgyal po zhig med na mi rung), or ťabs-méd (thabs med) Pth. we cannot do without a king; mťa-rten-méd-pai mťa (mtha rten med pai mtha) a termination with-out a final consonant Gram.; rgyu méd-par (rgyu med par) S.g. without cause; or by 'instead of': rgyál-po méd-par (rgyal po med par) Glr. instead of the king, sṅár-gyi lus méd-par (sngar gyi lus med par) Glr. instead of the former shape; nyin-mtsan-méd-par (nyin mtshan med par) making no difference between day and night, p̂o-mo-méd-par (pho mo med par) between male and female, rgan-byis-méd-par (rgan byis med par) old and young; vulgo also nyin-med-mtsán-med (nyin med mtshan med) etc. — méd-po (med po), W. *méd-k̥an* (*med kan*), fem. méd-mo (med mo), a poor man, pauper.
Mil. an ornament, piece of finery.
Ssk. मेंहदी, Lawsonia alba, a plant used for staining the finger-nails red Mil.
a kind of fur? mén-hri dmár-poi slóg-pa (men hri dmar poi slog pa) a fur-coat of red men-hri (men hri) is mentioned as the vesture of a Lha.

Cs.: 'a quaking; thinness; mér-po (mer po), mer-mér (mer mer) thin, as liquids'; Sch.: 'mér-gyis gaṅ (mer gyis gang) full to the brim'. I met with 1. mer (mer) in źig-mér (zhig mer) q.v. — 2. mér-ba (mer ba) as adj. for mtso (mtsho) the lake Mil. — 3. *mer-mér* (*mer mer*) W. adj. like a thin pap, and sbst. a muddy substance, e.g. street-mire; *mer-mér ċó-ċe* (*mer mer co ce*) to make a mire. — 4. mér-mer-ba (mer mer ba) adj in connection with such sbst. as light, ray, beam, brightness Thgr., Mil. — 5. mér-mer-po (mer mer po) used in medical writings in a similar manner as núr-nur-po (nur nur po), to define the shape of an embryo, oblong, oval; these descrip-tions, however, though partly founded on observation, are frequently very arbitrary, vague, and even contradictory. In W. the word has only the signification 3; a Lama from C. rendered it with 'full', which would agree with Sch. and no. 1, as well as with 'glittering, quivering', having some relation to no. 2 and no. 4.

1. watch, watch-man, sentinel; watcher, spy, mél-tse byéd-pa (mel tse byed pa) to watch, to keep watch Dzl.; jà-ra-mel-tse (ja ra mel tse) = *mel-tse* (*mel tse*) W. — 2. steatite or soap-stone, of a greenish colour.

vulgo *me-mé* (*me me*), grandfather; also forefather, ancestor, progenitor, saṅs-rgyás ťams-ċád-kyi spyí-mes kun-tu-bzaṅ-po (sangs rgyas thams cad kyi spyi mes kun tu bzang po) Thgr. Kuntuzanpo, the common progenitor of all the Buddhas; mes rgyál-po (mes rgyal po) Glr. merely equivalent to 'the old king';
